sentence_reviews.text: Reproducibility and continuous monitoring track versions, seeds, data, metrics, drift, incidents, and operational health across experiments and deployments.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF covers documentation, monitoring, drift/change management, incidents, and measurement; DDIA covers data-system versioning, logs, replication, operations, and monitoring concepts; Deep Learning/AIMA support experiment/evaluation context.
sentence_reviews.notes: The cited sources support the main technical relationship, but the wording combines atlas taxonomy terms rather than a single quoted source claim.

sentence_reviews.text: Coverage is necessarily incomplete: measured proxies, policies, and test suites cannot enumerate every deployment context or adversary. Its direct dependencies include Checkpointing, recovery & experiment state, Benchmarks, test suites & task metrics.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Reproducibility, drift & continuous monitoring is modeled as depending on Checkpointing, recovery & experiment state, Benchmarks, test suites & task metrics and feeding downstream nodes such as Evaluation-driven development & release gates, Observability, tracing & evaluation harnesses, Model registry, versioning & MLOps / LLMOps.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.
